policy-authoring Codex installation
===================================

Recommended for workspace teammates
-----------------------------------
Install the Codex plugin in the Codex app, open the plugin details, then use
Share to send it to teammates in the same ChatGPT/Codex workspace.

Reproducible CLI install
------------------------
codex plugin marketplace add yhgoon277/policy-authoring-skills --ref main
codex plugin add policy-authoring@policy-authoring-skills
codex plugin list

Direct skill-folder install
---------------------------
Unzip policy-authoring-skills-codex.zip. It contains five skill folders:

- policy-authoring-setup
- policy-detail-authoring
- policy-hierarchy-decomposition
- policy-integrity-audit
- policy-naming-readability

Copy all five folders into one of Codex's skill discovery paths:

- Personal: ~/.agents/skills/
- Repo/team: <your-project>/.agents/skills/

Then start a new Codex conversation and check that all five policy-* skills
are visible. The Python tools use only the standard library and read project
settings from policy_config.json.

Slash commands
--------------
Slash commands such as /policy-setup and /policy-audit are provided by the
Codex plugin package. They are available when installing the plugin through the
Codex marketplace flow. Direct skill-folder installs expose the skills, but not
the plugin slash commands.

Claude artifacts
----------------
Existing .claude-plugin files and the other dist/*.zip files are kept for
Claude / claude.ai. Codex uses the .codex-plugin manifest, the repo-local
.agents/plugins/marketplace.json, or direct .agents/skills copies.
